pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **I think "The Backpass Assassin" might need to re-evaluate his strategy because, by the looks of things, he’s passing himself back to the bottom of the standings.** With only 10 points and no real upsets under his belt, Gayden’s pick of Jordan as a fourth-tier team leaves folks scratching their heads — bless his heart. Zach, or "Mustard Boy," currently leading with 16 points, might seem like a genius for Switzerland and Ghana, but I have to say, those were more like throwing darts blindfolded and somehow hitting the board. Gunner, known here as "Bubba G," is sneaking up the standings thanks to France and Morocco’s solid starts, but don’t let that fool you; I’d hedge my bets on him flopping in the knockout stages. As for Devin, I feel for him, I really do, but when you’re banking on Haiti, it’s like trying to win a marathon wearing flip-flops. Lastly, we have Rafe, the "Noisemaker," who’s tied with Devin at 12 points, and I have to hand it to him: stumbling cluelessly into drafting Germany is the kind of happy accident you can't plan for. So, while "Mustard Boy" enjoys his moment in the sun, I suspect it won't be long until his luck runs drier than a saltine cracker.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy Secures Top Spot with Dominant Brazil Performance
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach claims Brazil's victory was entirely due to his draft strategy.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Climbs Standings with Morocco Win Against Hapless Scotland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G credits secret Moroccan tea for strategic brilliance.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper Vaults to Third as USA Breezes Past Australia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin says USA's two goals were easier than his fantasy draft.

pundit_takes.headline: The Backpass Assassin Set for Tactical Showdown with Ivory Coast
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden reportedly asked son Rafe the location of Ivory Coast on a map.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gayden bringing up the rear, ranked 5th; Father vs. Son just got real.
Rafe picked World Cup ghosts; bet he's scouting eSports teams next year.
Rafe owns Devin with a head-to-head differential of 6; that's a beatdown.
Gunner's surge knocks everyone's models sideways; teenager sends sims back to grade school.
